frameworks.name: decoded neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop fMRI multivoxel pattern reinforcement to alter neural representations

frameworks.name: multivoxel neuroreinforcement
frameworks.one_line: implicit operant pairing of latent target representation with reward

frameworks.name: exposure-based therapy
frameworks.one_line: conventional aversive exposure paradigm contrasted with nondistressing implicit alternative

core_question: Can multivoxel neuroreinforcement reduce amygdala threat responses to specific phobic stimuli without conscious exposure?
key_assumptions: implicit activation of target representation can be operantly reinforced
amygdala response indexes phobic threat signature
neural decoder reliably identifies target animal representations
manipulations: target vs control animal phobia assignment within subjects
double-blind randomized assignment to dose conditions
key_findings: significant decrease in amygdala response for target phobia post-treatment
no comparable decrease for control phobia (interaction effect)
no adverse events or dropouts reported
interpretations: multivoxel neuroreinforcement can specifically reduce threat signatures
intervention may complement conventional psychotherapy with nondistressing experience
